topic.name: Veterans and Community Support Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at supporting veterans, enhancing community service programs, and addressing social issues such as elder abuse and mental health.

Document 1: Amends the National and Community Service Act of 1990
increasing service opportunities for retiring professionals
Document 2: Directs the Attorney General to study and report to Congress on state laws and practices relating to elder abuse
Authorizes appropriations for FY2009-FY2015
Document 3: establish a program of awarding grants to states to assist veterans
provide reimbursement for costs associated with such course work
Document 4: Directs each military department Secretary to implement a program providing a Special Victims' Counsel
providing guidance and assistance for the military departments in addressing matters relating to sexual assault prevention
Document 5: authorizes the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to pay burial and funeral expenses
The VA may continue to pay educational assistance and subsistence allowances to eligible veterans
Document 6: prohibitions on the use of funds available to any U.S. agency
temporarily transfer Guantanamo detainees to DOD medical facilities
Document 7: allows administrative costs of the Service-Disabled Veterans' Insurance program to be paid through premiums
authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to furnish home improvements
Document 8: increase coordination and communication among federal, state, tribal, and local law enforcement agencies
prevent drug trafficking and reduce rates of alcohol and drug addiction
Document 9: establish a National Mental Health Policy Laboratory
evaluate and disseminate to such grantees evidence-based practices
Document 10: disapprove courses of education provided by a public institution of higher education that charges tuition and fees for veterans
extends from 12 to 17 years after discharge or release from active-duty service

topic.name: U.S. Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: This cluster discusses amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States, focusing on the suspension of duties for various products and materials.

Document 1: bans as a hazardous product any drop-side crib
used in public facilities such as hotels or day care centers
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on light emitting diode (LED) Tubular LED (TLED)
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on bisphenol A Bis(3-methacryloyloxypropyl) ether
Document 4: suspend temporarily the duty on diaper shells
water-resistant outer layer of laminated knitted fabric
Document 5: codify the terms 'in airtight containers'
promote public health and safety
Document 6: suspend temporarily the duty on certain sardines in oil
in airtight containers
Document 7: liquidate or reliquidate certain entries of large newspaper printing presses
Document 8: suspend temporarily the duty on 1,3-propanediaminium
polymer with 2-propenamide
Document 9: analyze annually the exchange rate policies of the People's Republic of China
impose additional tariffs
Document 10: suspend temporarily the duty on confectionery
containing synthetic sweetening agents

topic.name: Legislative Reforms and Accountability
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative reforms aimed at enhancing representation, accountability, and transparency in government operations.

Document 1: Considers the District of Columbia a congressional district for purposes of representation in the House of Representatives.
Limits the District to one Member under any reapportionment of Members.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Reserve Act to authorize each member of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to employ and select a maximum of four individuals for the member's staff.
The results of that vote must be made publicly available on the Board's website.
Document 3: Authorizes the National Commission on the BP Deepwater Horizon Oil Spill and Offshore Drilling to hold hearings, meet and act, take testimony and receive evidence.
Deems failure to obey such an order to be contempt of court.
Document 4: Requires Congress to ensure that all congressional documents and all votes cast in Congress are created, transmitted, and published in searchable electronic formats.
The Clerk of the House of Representatives and the Secretary of the Senate shall establish the Congressional Data Task Force.
Document 5: Amends the federal judicial code to require the Attorney General to report to Congress on certain authoritative Department of Justice legal interpretations.
Requires the Comptroller General to evaluate and report to Congress on the performance of the Office of Legal Counsel.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to redefine the term 'independent expenditure'.
Prescribes disclosure requirements for corporations, labor organizations, and certain other entities.
Document 7: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 to require each state to permit any eligible individual on the day of a federal election to register to vote.
Document 8: Directs the President to appoint one additional circuit judge for the second circuit court of appeals.
Reduces the number of circuit judges appointed to the District of Columbia circuit court of appeals.
Document 9: Divides the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit into two new circuits.
Authorizes the temporary assignment of circuit and district judges of the former Ninth Circuit between the new circuits.
Document 10: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to abolish the National Labor Relations Board.
Establishes within the Department of Justice a Bureau of Labor Relations Enforcement.

topic.name: Temporary Duty Suspensions
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States that involve temporary suspensions or reductions of duties on specific goods and materials.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on certain acrylic fiber tow.
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on mixtures of aminocyclopyrachlor and inert ingredients.
Document 3: reduce temporarily the duty on parts of frames and mountings for spectacles, goggles, or the like.
Document 4: suspend temporarily the duty on mixtures containing Thiencarbazone-methyl, Isoxadifen-ethyl, and Tembotrione.
Document 5: extend the temporary suspension of duty on fine animal hair of Kashmire (cashmere) goats.
Document 6: suspend temporarily the duty on women's sports bras of stretch fabric with textile or polymer-based electrodes.
Document 7: suspend temporarily the duty on certain acrylic synthetic staple fiber.
Document 8: extend the temporary suspension of duty on 2-methyl-5-nitrobenzenesulfonic acid.
Document 9: extend the temporary suspension of duty on Acetic acid, cyano[3-[(6-methoxy-2-benzothiazolyl)amino]-1H-isoindol-1-yl idene]-, pentyl ester.
Document 10: extend the suspension of duty on Styrene, ar-ethyl-, polymer with divinylbenzene and styrene beads with low ash.

topic.name: Federal Accountability and Intelligence Oversight
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative measures aimed at enhancing federal accountability in spending and oversight of intelligence activities.

Document 1: Requires reporting of federal funds usage to ensure transparency.
Mandates quarterly reports from recipients of federal funds.
Document 2: Amends FISA to impose stricter requirements on FBI's ability to collect business records.
Establishes new protocols for surveillance related to national security.
Document 3: Authorizes appropriations for intelligence activities across multiple federal agencies.
Specifies oversight mechanisms for intelligence funding.
Document 4: Details appropriations for intelligence-related activities, ensuring accountability in funding.
Includes provisions for personnel management within intelligence agencies.
Document 5: Amends customs laws to enhance enforcement and accountability in trade practices.
Requires investigations into claims of customs evasion.
Document 6: Establishes an Office of the Constitutional Advocate to protect individual rights in surveillance cases.
Directs judicial review processes for FISA Court decisions.
Document 7: Extends surveillance authorities under the USA PATRIOT Act, emphasizing oversight.
Revises definitions and requirements for intelligence investigations.
Document 8: Directs federal agencies on declassification processes to promote transparency.
Requires reporting on classification practices to Congress.
Document 9: Amends federal law to protect online communications and geolocation data.
Establishes warrant requirements for government access to electronic communications.
Document 10: Expands requirements for reporting breaches of health information privacy.
Mandates annual reporting on privacy violations to Congress.

topic.name: Medicare and Social Security Reforms
topic.description: Legislative amendments and acts aimed at improving Medicare services and Social Security benefits for various populations.

Document 1: Amends part B (Supplementary Medical Insurance) of title XVIII (Medicare)
provides for a special enrollment period for individuals whose COBRA or retiree coverage terminates.
Document 2: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
Prohibits the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) from accepting a bid from an entity for an area unless it meets state licensure requirements.
Document 3: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to cover marriage and family therapist services
Authorizes marriage and family therapists and mental health counselors to develop discharge plans for post-hospital services.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to enter into chronic care improvement program operator agreements
development, testing, evaluation, and implementation of severe mental illness chronic care improvement demonstration programs.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to issue a benefit guarantee certificate to each individual entitled to benefits under title II
represents the obligation of the federal government to provide for payment of OASDI benefits.
Document 6: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) to require physicians referring an individual for imaging
Requires the referring physician to provide the referred individual a written list of suppliers.
Document 7: Authorizes an increase of $150 in Social Security benefits for one month in 2010
Amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) (OASDI) to require an annual adjustment.
Document 8: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974
prohibit a group health plan from imposing any preexisting condition exclusion for individuals who have not attained 19 years of age.
Document 9: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to increase
the Medicare caps on the total number of full-time equivalent residents.
Document 10: Directs the Bureau of Labor Statistics of the Department of Labor to prepare and publish a monthly Consumer Price Index for Elderly Consumers
Amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) to use such new CPI in the computation of cost-of-living increases.

topic.name: Medicare Policy and Reform
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at improving Medicare services, coverage, and payment methodologies.

Document 1: establish a three-year demonstration project under part B of title XVIII of the Social Security Act
evaluate the benefits of providing payment for items and services needed for the in-home administration of intravenous immune globin
Document 2: Amends title XVIII of the Social Security Act
Modifies the Medicare payment rules for orthotics and prosthetics to account for supplier qualifications
Document 3: removes all limitations on COBRA continuation coverage
provides no deadline for discontinuing such coverage
Document 4: carry out a program of teleconsultation for the provision of remote mental health
modifies the Veterans Equitable Resource Allocation system to provide incentives for telemedicine
Document 5: reduce the coverage gap in prescription drug coverage under SSA part D
grants the Secretary of Health and Human Services authority to negotiate contracts with manufacturers
Document 6: define 'MRSA' and provide a list of best practices
requires acute care hospitals to screen patients entering intensive care units
Document 7: increase the number of diseases and conditions for which beneficiaries may be targeted for medication therapy management services
requires a Prescription Drug Plan sponsor to establish a process for identifying individuals
Document 8: repeals provisions requiring a health plan to submit a report concerning the ratio of the incurred loss to earned premiums
repeals a requirement that each U.S. hospital establish and make public a list of its standard charges
Document 9: amends part C of Medicare to terminate the permission to disenroll
restores the option under previous law to elect to change from an MA plan to the original Medicare fee-for-service plan
Document 10: prohibits the Secretary of Health and Human Services from using any recommendation of the Preventive Services Task Force to deny or restrict coverage
requires the sponsor of a group health plan to consult the medical guidelines and recommendations of relevant professional medical organizations

topic.name: Food and Drug Safety Regulation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at ensuring the safety and quality of food, drugs, and environmental health standards.

Document 1: establish a program to strengthen federal activities for ensuring that commercially distributed seafood meets federal food quality and safety requirements
increase the number of laboratories certified to Food and Drug Administration (FDA) standards.
Document 2: require foreign manufacturers and producers to register an agent in the United States
subjects to certain penalties an importer who fails to provide this or files a false declaration.
Document 4: revise sanctions for laboratories that intentionally refer proficiency testing samples required for certification
giving the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) discretion to substitute intermediate sanctions.
Document 5: require a public water system that detects an unregulated contaminant to be monitored
authorize the data contained in the national drinking water contaminant occurrence data base to be used by the public.
Document 7: prohibit the sale of liquid formulations of over-the-counter drugs that are not packaged with a dosage delivery device
allows the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to waive the requirement for metric units.
Document 10: require an application for approval for a new drug or biological product to include a proposed risk evaluation and mitigation strategy
establish a Drug Safety Oversight Board.

topic.name: U.S. Science and Environmental Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative acts related to science, technology, environmental protection, and international cooperation on climate change.

Document 1: America COMPETES Act to enhance science and technology initiatives in the U.S.
Directs the President to convene a National Science and Technology Summit.
Document 2: Establishes an Office on Global Climate Change within the Department of State.
Directs USAID to promote clean and efficient energy technologies in developing countries.
Document 3: Extends duty-free treatment for products from sub-Saharan African countries under AGOA.
Amends the Trade Act of 1974 to support trade preferences.
Document 4: Directs the President to establish a policy to assist sub-Saharan Africa with electricity access.
Prioritizes loan guarantees for African power projects.
Document 5: Authorizes EPA to make loans for water infrastructure projects.
Defines eligible projects for improving water efficiency and environmental sustainability.
Document 6: Amends commodity programs to support agricultural conservation efforts.
Adjusts payments based on conservation reserve contracts.
Document 7: Establishes the Supply Star program to promote energy-efficient supply chains.
Directs the Secretary of Energy to enhance supply chain efficiency.
Document 8: Prescribes safety and environmental protection requirements for pipeline transportation.
Defines penalties for major consequence violations.
Document 9: Establishes the Citrus Disease Research and Development Trust Fund for combating diseases in citrus production.
Requires notification before trade agreements affecting citrus duties.
Document 10: Requires the establishment of a federal greenhouse gas registry by the EPA.
Mandates the creation of a domestic offset program to sequester GHGs.

topic.name: Employment and Labor Rights
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at protecting employee rights and improving workplace conditions, including pay equity, employment suitability, immigration-related protections, and family leave policies.

Document 1: Amends the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 (FLSA) known as the Equal Pay Act to revise remedies for, enforcement of, and exceptions to prohibitions against sex discrimination in the payment of wages.
Prohibits retaliation for inquiring about, discussing, or disclosing the wages of the employee or another employee.
Document 2: Establishes criteria to determine whether an individual is suitable for employment with the District of Columbia government.
Prohibits a hiring authority from making a new determination under this Act for an individual who has already been determined suitable or fit based on character or conduct.
Document 7: Requires certain employers to permit each employee to earn at least 1 hour of paid sick time for every 30 hours worked.
Prohibits an employer from interfering with an employee's exercise of such rights.
Document 8: Authorizes the Secretary of Labor to award competitive grants to eligible entities to hire unemployed individuals age 16 and older.
Requires that priority in the award of grants be given to individuals who have been unemployed for more than a year.

topic.name: Cybersecurity and Small Business Development
topic.description: This cluster addresses legislation related to cybersecurity measures and the support and development of small businesses in the United States.

Document 1: Defines 'cybersecurity' as 'information security' which is defined...
Requires the President to initiate a rulemaking to establish a procedure for the designation...
Document 2: Amends the Small Business Act relating to small business development centers (SBDCs)...
Directs the Administrator to establish SBDC grant programs for: access to credit and capital...
Document 3: Amends the Small Business Investment Act of 1958 to increase the maximum amounts of outstanding leverage...
Requires each SBIC to certify that not less than 25% of the aggregate dollar amount of its financings will be provided to smaller enterprises.
Document 4: Amends the Small Business Act to reauthorize for FY2010-FY2012 the following programs for small businesses...
Requires the Director of the Office of Women's Business Ownership to administer programs and services...
Document 8: Requires the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to implement...
DHS must also seek to ensure participation of at least one information sharing and analysis organization...

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Human Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures aimed at addressing human rights, security, and international relations with various countries.

Document 1: Prohibits the federal government from providing the government of Vietnam with nonhumanitarian development, trade, economic, and security assistance during any fiscal year that exceeds the amount of such assistance provided during FY2009, unless such increases are matched or exceeded by additional funding for programs focusing on human rights and democracy promotion.
Expresses the sense of Congress regarding diplomacy with the government of Vietnam.
Document 2: Directs the President to designate a country as a 'country of conflict concern' if its government or anti-government forces are allowing one or more foreign terrorist organizations to engage in armed conflict.
Terminates such a designation when the President certifies to Congress that either or both of these circumstances no longer apply.
Document 3: States that it is U.S. policy to assist the government of Ukraine in restoring its sovereignty and territorial integrity.
Directs the President to impose three or more specified sanctions against entities that support destabilization efforts in Ukraine.
Document 4: Includes Israel in the visa waiver program upon the Secretary of Homeland Security's determination that Israel cooperates with the U.S. government on counterterrorism initiatives.
Requires that reciprocal privileges are extended to all U.S. citizens.
Document 5: Directs the President to ensure coordination of counternarcotics programs among relevant agencies, including the Department of State and the Department of Defense.
Prohibits any U.S. government international counternarcotics or anti-crime foreign assistance-related activity from being implemented without the Secretary of State's approval.
Document 6: Prohibits the use of funds made available to the Department of Defense to transfer any individual detained at Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay, Cuba, unless DOD certifies to Congress that the individual no longer poses a threat.
Amends the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2016.
Document 7: Prohibits a U.S. government grant, contract, or cooperative agreement for the reconstruction of Iraq to be awarded unless the government of Iraq provides at least 50% matching funds from non-U.S. government contributions.
Document 10: Establishes the Commission on Wartime Treatment of European Americans to review U.S. government wartime treatment of European Americans and European Latin Americans.
Directs the Commission to include reviews of the rationale for U.S. government's refusal to allow entry into the United States of Jewish and other refugees fleeing persecution.

topic.name: Tax Reform and Incentives
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at reforming tax codes and providing tax incentives for businesses and individuals.

Document 1: Preserving Consumer Health Accounts Act of 2016
exempt from the excise tax on excess benefit employer-sponsored health care plans
Document 2: Small Business Tax Cut Act
tax deduction equal to 20% of the lesser of qualified domestic business income or taxable income
Document 3: Guard and Reserve Financial Stability Act of 2008
business tax credit for 50 percent of the lesser of actual compensation paid to each Ready Reserve-National Guard employee
Document 4: Family Fairness and Opportunity Tax Reform Act
reduce to two the number of individual income tax brackets
Document 5: Church Plan Clarification Act of 2015
amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the tax treatment of church pension plans
Document 6: Eagle Employers Act
allow a taxpayer certified as an Eagle employer a tax credit for one percent of such employer's taxable income
Document 7: Corporate Tax Fairness Act
amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the taxation of the foreign-source income of domestic corporations
Document 8: Gulf Coast Oil Recovery Zone Tax Relief and Economic Recovery Act
non-recognition, for income tax purposes, of proceeds or payments from insurance received in connection with the Gulf oil spill
Document 9: Small Business Expensing Expansion and Permanency Act of 2007
increase the expensing allowance for small business assets to $200,000
Document 10: Paying a Fair Share Act of 2012
require an individual taxpayer whose adjusted gross income exceeds $1 million to pay a minimum tax rate of 30%

topic.name: Legislative Actions and Reforms
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative measures aimed at addressing crime victims' rights, honoring historical figures, and amending federal laws related to trade, education, and national security.

1: require the judge in the trial of every criminal case to read in open court the rights of crime victims
4: modifies procedures for the payment of restitution and monetary assessments by defendants in certain child pornography cases
establishes a Child Pornography Victim's Fund
2: Directs the Postal Service to provide for the issuance and sale of a semipostal
8: prohibits an officer or employee of the United States from issuing a national security letter
unless the letter is submitted to a designated judge
9: authorizes the Secretary of the Army to place in Arlington National Cemetery an American Braille tactile flag
honoring blind members of the Armed Forces
10: extends the grant program for armor vests for law enforcement officers
amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968

topic.name: Public Health Initiatives
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving public health through various programs and initiatives.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to revise and expand the program to reduce underage drinking
expressing the sense of Congress that the age-21 minimum drinking law has been an effective public health and safety policy
Document 4: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Director of the National Institutes of Health (NIH) to expand and intensify NIH programs with respect to translational research
establish or expand training programs for medical and allied health clinicians and scientists in research relevant to Down syndrome
Document 5: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Director of the Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality to conduct and support research, evaluations, initiatives, and demonstration projects
enhance the deployment of medical simulation technologies and the incorporation of such technologies into medical education and training protocols
Document 6: Amends the Public Health Service Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to develop and implement a plan for the prevention, control, and management of hepatitis C virus (HCV)
conduct epidemiologic research to identify best practices for HCV prevention
Document 10: Amends the Public Health Service Act to expand the scope and responsibilities of the United States Preventive Services Task Force
publish research plans to guide its review of scientific evidence relating to the effectiveness of preventive services

topic.name: Energy Policy and Regulation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures aimed at regulating energy production, promoting renewable energy, and addressing environmental impacts associated with fossil fuels.

Document 1: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act and the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to repeal the authority of the Secretary of the Interior to reduce or eliminate royalty payments for oil and natural gas leases.
Terminates the Office of Fossil Energy Research and Development in DOE and the authority to carry out any of its programs.
Document 2: Amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to renewable energy and other energy-related tax incentives.
Extends eligibility for clean renewable energy bonds (CREBs) and the issuance authority for such bonds through 2018.
Document 3: Establishes within the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) a WaterSense program to identify and promote water efficient products, buildings and landscapes, and services to reduce water use, conserve energy, and preserve water resources.
Document 4: Amends the Energy Independence and Security Act of 2007 to repeal the prohibition against any federal agency contract for procurement of an alternative or synthetic fuel.
Requires that lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions associated with the production and combustion of the fuel must be less than or equal to those from conventional petroleum sources.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to replace the tax credit for qualified alternative fuel vehicle refueling property expenditures with a tax credit for 50% of the cost of any qualified electric vehicle recharging or refueling property.
Document 6: Increases civil penalties for failure to take corrective action pertaining to drilling violations on federal land.
Authorizes the Department of the Interior to increase such penalties after notice and an opportunity for public comment.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Energy to study and report to Congress with recommendations for appropriate methods and standards for requiring motor vehicle fuel suppliers to disclose information regarding each country in which the fuel was processed.
Requires the Secretary to prescribe regulations requiring disclosure of country-of-origin information by motor vehicle fuel suppliers.
Document 8: Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 by requiring the Department of Energy (DOE) to carry out the Solar Fuels Basic Research Initiative to expand scientific knowledge about converting solar energy into chemical energy.
Supports research about replicating natural photosynthetic processes in artificial photosynthesis processes.
Document 9: Amends the Natural Gas Act to repeal the exclusive authority of the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to approve or deny an application for the siting, construction, expansion, and operation of liquefied natural gas (LNG) terminals.
Directs FERC to require such application to identify each employee or agent engaged in activities to persuade communities of the benefits of the approval.
Document 10: Authorizes additional FY2011-FY2012 appropriations for formula grants to both urbanized and nonurbanized areas for the operating and capital costs of public transportation including intercity bus service.
Allows a tax credit for new qualified heavy natural gas or hybrid motor vehicles.

topic.name: Financial Relief Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing financial relief and protections for individuals and businesses facing economic distress.

Document 1: Amends federal bankruptcy law to cite circumstances under which a medically distressed debtor may elect to exempt from the property of the estate in bankruptcy
Denies a discharge in bankruptcy from any debt incurred that relates to attorneys' fees generated as a result of the debtor's filing of a Chapter 7 petition.
Document 2: stabilize the housing market and assist homeowners by imposing a temporary moratorium on foreclosures
creating tax and other incentives to help prevent foreclosures and encourage refinancing into affordable and sustainable mortgage solutions.
Document 3: Prohibits a creditor, during a specified nine-month period, from increasing any annual percentage rate
Authorizes the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to prescribe implementing regulations.
Document 5: Establishes special rules for modification or disposition of a troubled asset
Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to establish and implement a home mortgage loan relief program under TARP.
Document 9: authorizes the Department of the Treasury to establish a temporary three-year program to facilitate federal student loan refinancing
allows an exclusion from gross income for the payment of an employer, either to an employee or a lender, of any indebtedness of an employee under a qualified education refinance loan.

topic.name: Land Management and Conservation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and actions related to the management, conveyance, and conservation of federal and state lands, including habitat protection and land exchanges.

Document 1: modification of producer and operator conservation reserve program contracts
allow harvesting or grazing on agricultural lands
Document 2: require proceeds from sales to be used for habitat conservation plan
extends withdrawal and purchase authority
Document 3: directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey land
limits the use of land to traditional and customary uses
Document 4: release, convey, and quitclaim to the state of Florida
requires state to use proceeds for land acquisition
Document 5: exchange of federal and nonfederal lands
designating a road corridor through the refuge
Document 6: designates lands as wilderness
establishes biological connecting corridors
Document 7: directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey land
requires fair market value payment
Document 8: convey to the city all interest of the United States in such land
requires appraisal conducted according to recognized standards
Document 9: directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey parcels of National Forest System land
requires use of land for public purposes only
Document 10: directs the USDA to convey federal land to the Trust Authority
complete any necessary land surveys and required pre-exchange clearances

topic.name: Career and Technical Education Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing and funding career and technical education programs and teacher preparation in the United States.

Document 1: reauthorizes the Carl D. Perkins Career and Technical Education Act
increases funding reserved for career and technical education activities in rural areas
Document 2: prohibits the Secretary of Education from issuing a final rule affecting postsecondary career education programs
Document 3: authorizes grants for career and technical education teacher preparation programs
requires performance measures for career and technical education teacher preparation programs
Document 4: makes individuals eligible to apply for loans under the William D. Ford Federal Direct Loan program for educational programs leading to a degree or certificate
Document 5: establishes a competitive demonstration grant program to increase the number of school nurses in public schools
Document 6: authorizes competitive grants to establish American Dream Accounts for low-income public school students
Document 7: creates Summer of Service programs providing community service learning opportunities to youth
Document 8: requires institutions to include policies regarding harassment in their annual security report
Document 9: allows LEAs to use funds for educational activities for children four years of age or younger
Document 10: directs the Secretary of Education to allot grants to states to offer Dreamer students in-state tuition

topic.name: Housing and Urban Development Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative measures aimed at improving housing assistance, mortgage practices, and employment opportunities related to housing and urban development.

Document 1: Fair Mortgage Practices Act of 2007 - Prohibits any person, with certain exceptions, from engaging in the business of loan origination without first obtaining and maintaining a registration or a state license as a loan originator.
Amends the Truth in Lending Act to set forth: (1) disclosure requirements for consumer credit plans secured by the consumer's principal dwelling.
Document 2: Amends the Native American Housing Assistance and Self-Determination Act of 1996 to give the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) 60 days to act before the request of an Indian tribe for a waiver is deemed approved.
Makes tribal prevailing wage laws applicable to the administration of all federal funding for projects funded under the Act.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to make grants and loans to qualified states, metropolitan cities, and urban counties, in accordance with HUD-approved plans, to carry out eligible housing stimulus activities.
Requires such program to be administered through HUD's Office of Community Planning and Development.
Document 10: Amends the National Housing Act (NHA) to direct the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to establish and collect additional annual premium payments for the first 11 years of the term of an insured mortgage.
Directs the Secretary to establish a process for mortgagees to appeal indemnification determinations.

topic.name: Federal Legislation and Designations
topic.description: This cluster discusses various federal acts and designations related to buildings, resources, and management practices.

Document 1: Amends the FDA Food Safety Modernization Act
permits the continued exemption from the Act
Document 2: Designates the federal building and U.S. courthouse
located at 515 West First Street in Duluth, Minnesota
Document 3: Great Lakes Maritime Heritage Assessment Act of 2014
Requires the Under Secretary for Oceans and Atmosphere to assess and report
Document 4: Amends the Foreign Affairs Reform and Restructuring Act of 1998
reauthorize the United States Advisory Commission on Public Diplomacy
Document 5: Designates the federal building and U.S. courthouse located at 1300 Victoria Street
as the 'George P. Kazen Federal Building and United States Courthouse'
Document 6: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service
as the 'Jacob M. Lowell Post Office Building'
Document 7: designates the Department of Veterans Affairs temporary lodging facility
as the 'Dr. Otis Bowen Veteran House'
Document 8: Shuttle Workforce Revitalization Act of 2011
be treated as a HUBZone for purposes of that Act
Document 9: Fishery Management Transparency and Accountability Act
direct each Regional Fishery Management Council to make available on the Internet
Document 10: Directs the Chief of the Army Corps of Engineers to revise the Missouri Mainstem Reservoir System Master Water Control Manual
delete fish and wildlife as an authorized purpose of the Corps

topic.name: Veterans Affairs Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the benefits and healthcare services provided to veterans and their families.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to reimburse certain veterans for emergency treatment received in a non-VA facility.
Reimbursement for customary and usual charges of emergency treatment for veterans.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to establish and maintain a special record for individuals stationed at Canadian Forces Base Gagetown.
Requires the Secretary to provide health examinations and counseling.
Document 3: Permits an eligible beneficiary to continue TRICARE Prime coverage if residing farther than 100 miles from a military medical treatment facility.
Makes this Act effective as if included in the enactment of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2014.
Document 4: Amends the Energy Employees Occupational Compensation Program Act of 2000 to improve occupational illness compensation for survivors.
Requires payment to survivors of deceased contractor employees who applied for compensation.
Document 5: Directs the Department of Veterans Affairs to increase rates of veterans' disability compensation and related benefits as of December 1, 2016.
Increase shall be the same percentage as benefits provided under the Social Security Act.
Document 6: Allows individuals eligible for TRICARE to elect to be ineligible for enrollment and make tax-deductible contributions to a health savings account.
DOD shall provide information regarding health savings accounts to TRICARE-eligible individuals.
Document 7: Establishes the Merchant Mariner Equity Compensation Fund for payments to WWII merchant mariners.
Requires individuals to apply for benefits within one year after the enactment of this Act.
Document 8: Extends the time limitation for the use of educational assistance under the Montgomery GI Bill.
Allows individuals who completed at least 50% of credit requirements to extend their eligibility.
Document 9: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to ensure VA orthotic-prosthetic laboratories are certified.
Requires development of a plan to inform disabled veterans of technological advances in prosthetics.
Document 10: Amends the Higher Education Act to allow service members to defer student loan payments during military service.
Allows deferment for loans when a call to duty is cancelled.

topic.name: Legislation for Public Safety and Rights
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing public safety, protecting individual rights, and regulating commerce.

Document 1: Amends the Hobbs Act to authorize imposition of a fine of up to $100,000, 20 years imprisonment, or both for obstructing, delaying, or affecting commerce
Makes provisions regarding any such interference inapplicable to conduct that is incidental to otherwise peaceful picketing during the course of a labor dispute.
Document 2: Deems human life to exist from conception, without regard to race, sex, age, health, defect, or condition of dependency
Removes Supreme Court and district court jurisdiction to review cases arising out of any statute protecting the rights of human persons between conception and birth.
Document 3: Imposes a fine and/or prison term of up to 5 years on any business entity or product supervisor who fails to inform of a serious danger
Imposes a fine and/or prison term of up to 1 year on any individual who intentionally discriminates against an employee who warns of a danger.
Document 4: Prohibits knowingly and willfully introducing or delivering for introduction into interstate commerce a product that is labeled as maple syrup but that is not maple syrup
Defines 'maple syrup' to mean a liquid food derived by concentration and heat treatment of the sap.
Document 5: Establishes a certification process with respect to nondisclosure requirements imposed in connection with specified administrative subpoenas
Creates a judicial review process with respect to the imposition of such requirements.
Document 6: Defines 'authorized reseller' as any person authorized by a manufacturer to sell mobile devices
Subjects a violator to fine, imprisonment, or both for selling pre-paid mobile devices without authorization.
Document 7: Prohibits selling or disseminating the wireless telephone information of any individual without prior written authorization
Allows more stringent state laws.
Document 8: Makes it unlawful for any person to use a civil unmanned aircraft system (drone) to willfully conduct surveillance of another person without consent
Prescribes penalties for violations of these requirements.
Document 9: Requires an abortion provider to inform a woman of the probable age of the child before performing an abortion
Establishes civil penalties for willfully failing to comply with this Act.
Document 10: Prohibits knowingly selling or maintaining an adult advertisement designed to induce a commercial exchange for a sexual act
Requires verification of identity for each person purchasing advertisement space.

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Conservation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, preservation, and development of federal lands and natural resources in the United States.

Document 1: Designates specified federal lands in the Monongahela National Forest, West Virginia, as wilderness
Requires the Secretary to develop a plan to provide for enhanced nonmotorized recreation trail opportunities
Document 2: Modifies the project for navigation, Mississippi River Ship Channel, Gulf of Mexico to Baton Rouge, Louisiana
Consult with appropriate federal, state, and local agencies and stakeholders to determine the safe placement and timing
Document 3: Enter into cooperative agreements with non-federal public and private entities to provide for the maintenance, development, or construction of recreation facilities
Permit any such entity to collect fees for the use of recreation sites and facilities
Document 4: Declares that a presidential permit shall not be required for the pipeline described in the application filed
Deems the final environmental impact statement issued by the Secretary of State on August 26, 2011
Document 5: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to provide financial and technical assistance to the Eastern New Mexico Rural Water Authority
Prohibits the use of funds under this Act until such plan is developed
Document 6: Directs the Bureau of Land Management to establish priority and variance areas on covered land for geothermal, solar, and wind energy projects
Establishes in the Treasury the Renewable Energy Resource Conservation Fund
Document 7: Requires the U.S. Forest Service to coordinate with the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), state and local agencies
The bill excludes certain activities that reduce forest fuels in the basin management unit from the requirements of NEPA
Document 8: Amends the District of Columbia Appropriation Act, 1982, as amended by the District of Columbia Appropriations Act, 1990
Authorizes the Agency to lease or sell the Site to a redevelopment company or other lessee or purchaser
Document 9: Conduct forest management activities in the Lake Tahoe Basin in a manner that helps achieve and maintain the environmental threshold carrying capacities
Requires the Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit to manage vehicular parking and traffic
Document 10: Consolidates and amends three federal programs that provide payments to local governments that have federal lands within their borders
Provides permanent mandatory funding for the PILT program

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at modifying tax provisions related to individuals, businesses, and specific groups.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code
extend through December 31, 2017, the income tax credit
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code
exempt from employment taxes combat zone compensation
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code
treat firefighting and prevention services as a charitable contribution
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code
increase from 70 1/2 to 75 the age at which beneficiaries must begin taking distributions
Document 5: amends the Internal Revenue Code
make permanent the tax rule that reduces the basis of the stock
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code
increase by $500 million in 2008 the new markets tax credit limitation amount
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code
repeal certain exceptions to the treatment of consolidated groups
Document 8: Amends the Internal Revenue Code
repeal a provision that extends to corporations the requirement to report payments
Document 9: Amends the Internal Revenue Code
allow caregivers a refundable income-based tax credit
Document 10: Amends the Internal Revenue Code
exclude from gross income amounts attributable to the discharge of certain residential mortgage obligations
